reviews.text: It is my pleasure to submit this 5-star review for Ettinger Law Firm. Several months ago, my wife and I began to look for an elder care law firm to handle our Trust and Will. We spoke to friends and family members who went through this process. We interviewed several lawyers and law firms who specialize in elder care Trusts & Wills. We did our homework and asked them many questions. Most of the attorneys we interviewed seemed qualified and answered our questions. Most law firms had similar legal fees for the work we required. After each interview, my wife and I evaluated the lawyers' responses and what their reccomendations were. The last law firm we interviewed was Ettinger. We requested a Zoom with Michelle Berman. Michelle was terrific. She answered all of our questions and many more. She also explained in detail the differences between Revocable and Irrevocable trusts and recommended what she thought would be best for us. After we decided to go with Ettinger, we exchanged many emails  phone calls and Zooms with Michelle. It took us several weeks of going back and forth before we completed everything that is a part of our Trust & Will documents. I highly recommend Michelle Berman of Ettinger Law Firm.  She was very knowledgeable, well informed, and a great attorney. I also want to acknowledge how courteous their office staff were. Most of all, Maria was terrific.  If you are looking to hire a law firm for your family, Trust and Will needs you will be in good hands with Ettinger Law.
Michelle, Maria and Oliver were all great and very helpful

The Ettinger team at the White Plains, NY office Michelle Berman, Oliver Sauter, Maria Roman and Mr Ettinger were very knowledgeable, respectful and professional.     We were treated very well and are very pleased with our experience in obtaining our Irrevocable Trust and wills in order.

We feel comfortable in recommending them to others seeking professional services in getting their estate affairs.

Ms. Claro is knowledgeable, responsive, and truly effective at what she does. reviews.text: Best Lawyer in the tri state!!! Thank you again Mr. Bartlett for the successful outcome!!!

We needed legal counsel and we found Mr. Bartlett to be excellent.  He was a pleasure to work with, extremely knowledgeable and we would highly recommend him to those seeking legal expertise.  10 stars in our book!

Mr. Bartlett is simply outstanding. His deep understanding of the legal system and communication skills are truly amazing. He navigated a very tough time for me and secured the complete dismissal of my case. Without a doubt, having him as my lawyer was the best experience I could have asked for.

Knowledgeable, heartfelt advise. If he can assist in your case he will, if not will be upfront and let you know he can't. He will take the time to inform and explain possible paths to take. Highly recommend!
Mr. Bartlett is knowledgeable and communicates with you every step of the way. He was a God sent.

Mr.Bartlett is a man of the upmost integrity. Not only is he an excellent role model but his sound advice and compassion helped me keep my head on my shoulders in one of my most perilous moments. With his vast experience and sound judgement he was able to help me navigate what could’ve been a very detrimental situation at a very vulnerable time in my life. Mr. Bartlett advocated and fought for me like I was a member of the family and he was able to get my case dismissed with all the charges dropped. I’m indebted to this man and if you need someone who will fight for you advocate for you and help you navigate a perilous legal situation Mr. Bartlett is the one you should call!

I decided to hire Mr. Bartlett for my DWI case after he was referred to me by multiple people as I searched for an attorney.  I am so happy I did. He was always reachable and got back to me within minutes. First, we had a DMV refusal hearing with the DMV court which he was remarkably able to get dismissed !!!! This was huge for me because no matter the outcome of the DWI case my license would’ve been suspended for a year.  Next after months of going back and forth and the prosecution not offering me a favorable deal we decided to go to trial.  During the trial, Mr. Bartlett was outstanding and got the CASE DISMISSED !!! I couldn’t have asked for a better outcome and lawyer.  I highly recommend Mr. Bartlett.
The worst lawyer my wife treated did not say anything and charged money. Alfinar lost the case and never remembered anything.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I don’t believe I ever represented you or your wife. I believe you are mistaken sir. Best wishes.

Sincerely,


Mayo Bartlett

reviews.text: Swrious, caring ,competent, a heart that doesn't come with a degree, a very good man, Mayo Bartlett Esq.

Amazing attorney. Mr. Bartlett could not represent me, due to a conflict of interest, but just his professionalism, kindness, and frankly, amazing empathy is noteworthy of this review. To all those who are represented by him: you're lucky! I wish I could retain him and if I ever have a case where there isn't a conflict: this is my attorney. I've dealt with a great deal of lawyers, but he is second-to-none.
If you're looking for a knowledgeable, respected, well connected attorney this is the man you want. Very friendly, prompt and answerws your calls! Excellent results.

During our time of extreme stress, Mr. Bartlett made a difficult process much easier to get through. He always made himself available for questions and concerns. He is a true professional with extensive knowledge and experience. I would recommend the Law Offices of Mayo Bartlett, PLLC to family and friends.

Attorney Mayo Bartlett represented me in a difficult case that many other lawyers told me it’d be impossible to get any favorable results. The case turned out to be more work than either of us imagined but Mr. Bartlett remained confident and eager to do what is best for me. Mr. Bartlett never requested any additional payment outside of the original retainer agreement, he was always communicative and upfront with me. Throughout the case I’ve learned that Mayo Bartlett is well respected by many of the other professionals that I’ve encountered. After the disposition, I had the judge tell me what a great person and attorney Mayo Bartlett is. I am thankful for Mayo and his tireless work for my case and getting me the results 4 other attorneys said was impossible.

It was a pleasure working with Mayo Bartlett. He was very knowledgeable and communicative throughout our entire time working with one another. I highly recommend his services - You're in good hands with Mr. Bartlett!
Mr. Barlett is professional, understanding and takes serious interest in your case.  I highly recommend.
Very professional explains every step of the way with compassion and patience. Responds very quickly to calls and texts. If you have any legal issues he is the man.

Amazing law firm
Very professional
Mayo has handled my legal affairs for over 20 years.  He is a consummate professional and a compassionate member of the legal community.  I refer everyone to him and he deserves more than 5 stars!
Beautiful and Clean👍

Mayo Bartlett counseled me about an aggravated harassment case I’ve been dealing with (I am pressing charges). Anyone who has gone through something like this can understand the emotional toll it can take on someone. Having his HONEST, practical and kind advice was invaluable during this difficult time. Even though he advised me not to seek his counsel at this time, I will CERTAINLY refer him to others and if necessary in the future will utilize his services. I really appreciate you Mr. Bartlett.

I have had an extremely successful professional as well as friendly relationship over 15 years or so with Mr. Bartlett. To say he has been extremely helpful is an understatement even if it was to give clarification on the most simple questions. The knowledge he has and has passed along to myself to help understand our legal system, I feel no place else could have given the same respect. Very much highly recommend Mr. Bartlett and his team for any situation even the most minuscule of problems. He is a crucial cornerstone in having a strong legal team and advice. Thank you as always Mr. B

Will

I just found this Lawyers info online to give to my brother who is in need of a Lawyer and Either Mayo Bartlett picked up or someone from his office and after my brother explained his problem their response was "Stick to your legal aid because you can not afford me." How Rude and unprofessional.   For you to be a civil rights Lawyer you sure aren't here to help the African American people. I don't know about his skills but if he conducts hisself in the courtroom the way he did on this call I'm glad I won't find out. We come from a family of hard workers and would put our monies together if necessary for my brothers freedom. I am appalled and disgusted. Will not refer this firm to anyone.
Honest professional that puts 100% into his work and all of  his cases and truly cares for his clients. Has true  knowledge and integrity outstanding lawyer.
Mayo is the best he really helped me out in my time of need
A great lawyer. A better man.
Attorney Bartlett is extremely competent and well versed in all manners of criminal law.  It was an absolute pleasure to work with this extremely professional person.

I highly recommend Mayo.  He’s a talented attorney with integrity & compassion.  He fights hard for all those who he represents.  He’s a former prosecutor so he knows all aspects of the law & how the system works.
Mr. Bartlett was Compassionate and very well informed about my situation, even though I contacted him at the last minute. He made the process of navigating through this legal system simple.

I don't have enough words to describe how amazing the experience with Mayo Bartlett was. He work's very hard to make sure you have winning results. I could not have asked for better services. From the moment I first called until my case was over, I was at ease that I has made the right choice. Not to mention Mayo is highly respected and well known. I highly recommend.

Peter Spino, Jr. is an outstanding real estate attorney. He was always available, incredibly knowledgeable about every aspect of the law, and took the time to explain each step in detail. His compassion, patience, and professionalism made the entire process stress-free, and thanks to his expertise, the property sale was successful. I couldn’t recommend Peter Spino, Jr. and his law office more highly.
Peter did a solid job with the purchase of my condo. I was a first time homebuyer and would recommend him to anyone in need of a real estate attorney.

Great communication from beginning to end. Peter never hesitated to answer any questions and concerns that I had. He was very thorough and inspired much confidence throughout my home purchasing journey!

My husband and I had the pleasure of working with Peter on two separate real estate transactions — the purchase of our first home and the sale of our co-op apartment — and we couldn’t be happier with the service he provided.

Throughout both processes, Peter was extremely professional, responsive, and thorough. Whether by email or phone, he was always quick to answer our questions and made sure we understood every step. As first-time homebuyers, we especially appreciated how clearly he explained the contract, inspection process, bank requirements, and what to expect at closing. His guidance gave us confidence and peace of mind.

When it came time to sell our co-op, Peter once again walked us through the process seamlessly. He made sure we were fully informed about the co-op board’s requirements and what was needed for a successful closing.

We highly recommend Peter to anyone in need of a reliable and knowledgeable real estate attorney. We will absolutely use his services again in the future!

Peter Spino is very knowledgeable and hard working.  He puts forth 💯 effort to get the best result. It was a difficult case, but Peter
Kept me updated and settled the case as efficiently and transparently as possible. I would definitely use him again.
Thanks for all your hard work!

I had  the pleasure of working with Attorney Peter Spino on the sale of my house, and I couldn’t be more satisfied. His aggressive efforts and dedication made sure the transaction went through smoothly, even when challenges came up. He was always responsive, knowledgeable, and truly had my best interests in mind. Thanks to his hard work, everything was finalized without delays. I highly recommend Peter Spino to anyone looking for a reliable and results-driven attorney.
We used Peter for our closing and are very pleased with how everything was handled. He was very responsive to all questions and we'll definitely be using him again.

Difficult transaction is an understatement. Peter has worked with us since February and stayed with us when others would have suggested getting another attorney. We finally closed this week. He is an excellent attorney and a great person. Thank you, Peter!

Seamless transaction! I recently worked with Peter Spino on the sale of my parents’ property and had a great experience. He was very responsive and easy to communicate with throughout the entire process. Everything was clearly explained, and the transaction went smoothly without any issues. I appreciated his professionalism and would definitely recommend him to anyone in need of a real estate attorney.

Peter came highly recommended when I was in need of help during a foreclosure. He expertly navigated this difficult situation and ultimately counseled me thru a successful and profitable sale of my home.  Not only is he an expert in his field of law but he is a stand up guy who really cares about achieving a positive outcome for his clients.

Two years ago we used Peter on the purchase of a home for our daughter in Pelham.  Not being local we needed someone we could trust on this transaction.  His professionalism and integrity in that transaction was unparalleled.  This month we used Peter to sell the same home for us and as expected he did not disappoint.  I would not hesitate to use him again and highly recommend his firm to anyone needing expert real estate advice and services.
Jim Wade
Dallas, Texas
Peter is dependable & efficient which I really appreciated both with buying and selling a property. Highly recommend!

Peter is professional and personable. He takes the time to answer questions thoroughly and explains the entire process from contract to closing. My case had challenges before consulting with Peter and he saw the gaps and errors that needed to be corrected and his recommendations proved successful. I also appreciate the timely communication and follow up on my questions. Strongly recommend

It was a pleasure working with Peter he was recommended to me by my realtor and it was the best decision i made, very knowledgeable of real estate laws he explained things I wasn't familiar with and I was able to understand after he  explained to me very pleased  with his services. Highly recommend to anyone who needs a real estate lawyer
Did the impossible. Saved a mortgage from foreclosure with a notorious lender. I will be forever grateful.

As a full-time Realtor here in Westchester County for the last 10+ years, over time, many of my clients have asked me to recommend an Attorney to handle their purchase or sale. Lucky for them (and for me) that I came to know Peter very early on in my career as a Realtor. He is my "go-to" guy when a client needs an Attorney, and not one of the dozens of clients I have brought him has ever had anything but good things to say about him. Peter goes above and beyond in protecting my clients' best interests, keeps transactions moving smoothly, deals with unexpected problems effectively and promptly, and overall just gets things done ASAP, all the while maintaining a calm and pleasant demeanor. He is a pleasure to work with, and I am always happy when my clients act on my recommendation and engage Peter as their Attorney.  Five stars aren't high enough of a rating for him.
Worked with Peter to sell my house, he was always available and got the job done
Peter was very responsive and helpful during the process of purchasing and closing on a home. He was patient and thorough.
Amazing attorney with great attention to detail and expertise. Been working with Peter and team for years successfully with no error. Highly recommend.

Really love working with Peter. I’m a realtor and he has always been professional, helpful, hard working and straightforward with my clients and me. It’s something special to have an attorney who cares like him.
I've worked with Peter on a few deals. As a Realtor, I truly appreciate an attorney who communicates not only with myself, but with the clients I refer. Peter is a great attorney. Highly recommend.

Peter walked us through the process of buying our first home in Hartsdale, NY during the pandemic 2020. He and his team were always quick to respond and very knowledgeable about every step of the process. We highly recommend them.

Mr. Spino helped save my home from foreclosure. By negotiating with one of the toughest banks,  I was granted a loan modification w a 2% interest rate. Mr. Spino was God-sent.  He is attentive, empathetic, respectful, non-condescending, very supportive. I am forever grateful for his service.

In real estate, the attorney involved in the transaction can make or break the deal.  I am assured of a positive outcome every time with Peter Spino.  I have been working with Mr. Spino for a few years now and I am hard pressed to find anyone who is as accessible and consistent as he is.  There is the knowledge and professionalism that he  brings to his practice that is further enhanced by his compassion and diligence. In addition, because Mr. Spino has diversified his skills into other areas of law, he is an invaluable resource in a broad spectrum of legal matters.  For all the reasons here in stated I strongly recommend The Law Office of Peter Spino Jr.

I have had the pleasure of utilizing the services of Mr. Spino on different occasions, always with the very best of results. He is an intelligent professional and possesses rare qualities that are not readily present when dealing with lawyers. Integrity, Kindness, fairness and a great sense of humor.
Where other lawyers simply dabble, Peter Spino shines.

I have been working with Peter for the last few years and all I can say is that he consistently goes above and beyond what is expected of him, or any other lawyer for that matter. Every time I go to his office, he always makes time to sit with me and have a real conversation instead of just trying to get through everything as quickly as possible in a "time is money" fashion. Not only would I recommend Peter, but I already have. Needing a lawyer isn't usually a happy affair, but Peter always makes people feel better about their situation.
Peter is a very bright and attorney, who is a pro at what he does.   I refer clients to him frequently and never a complaint.  Highly recommended !

Excellent legal representation. Approachable and listens to your case without scolding you like a bad child! Gets the job done without holding your wallet hostage. Thank you for your help and hope to continue to have him as my counsel for property matters.

While going through a difficult time with my health. I ran into problems keeping up with my mortgage. I applied for a modification and the bank was not working in my best interest. They offered a modification without lowering my rate and actually increasing my payment. I had dealt with two law firms to no avail. I spent a lot of money for constant postponements.
A friend highly recommended Clair Cjertsen & Weathers PLLC from their own very positive experience. Mary Aufecht took my case and from day one she was amazing! She was professional and persistent with the bank. She pointed out the lack of cooperation from their end and was able to secure a modification with a big reduction in interest rate. This enabled me to get back on my feet. I will always be grateful for the dedication and hard work that was put into my case. They kept me updated every step of the way. What was a very difficult time in my life with a fear of losing my home, turned into a wonderful experience with a very professional team.  I was treated as my case was as important as every client. It restored my faith in attorneys actually getting the job done!
Now, I highly recommend this firm for any of your needs. You will not be disappointed!
Sincerely,
Frances E. Silipo

I had the privilege of working with Jennifer Swift and Nanette Saturn and I cannot recommend them highly enough. As a parent navigating the complexities of educational law for my child, I was feeling overwhelmed and unsure about how to advocate for his needs within the school system. We started our journey working with Nanette, an amazing educational advocate. As our journey became more complex, Jennifer Swift jumped on board as the attorney we needed to navigate our unique situation.  Nanette and Jennifer were not only knowledgeable but also incredibly empathetic.

One of the things that stood out most was how thoroughly they took the time to listen. They didn’t just focus on the legal aspects of our case but genuinely cared about what was best for my child. They took the time to break down all of our options and helped us make informed decisions, rather than simply pushing us toward the most expedient route.

What truly sets Barger and Gaines apart, however, is their deep expertise in educational law. They are clearly well-versed in the nuances of special education services, IEPs, and the rights of students within the school system. Their strategic advice and guidance were invaluable in navigating the intricacies of education policies. When it came time for negotiations with the school district, I felt confident knowing I had a strong attorney by my side who knew exactly how to approach the situation.

Throughout the process, Nanette and Jennifer were always responsive, patient, and professional. They promptly answered my questions, followed up regularly, and ensured that I was never left in the dark. It was clear that they genuinely cared about achieving the best outcome for my family, and their dedication was evident every step of the way.

Thanks to Barger and Gaines we were able to secure the support and services my child needed for his success in school. I left every meeting feeling more informed, empowered, and reassured. If you are looking for an advocate and/or attorney who not only understands the law but also understands the importance of education and advocacy, this firm is the one to turn to.

In short: compassionate, knowledgeable, and truly dedicated to their clients’ success. I’m incredibly grateful for the expertise and support they provided during such a critical time for our family.

￼ ￼I Retained Gregg for a Personal Injury Case Involving My Mom. He is A True Professional and was Attentive to all My Needs And Questions, always Returned Calls Promptly . Awesome experience!!!! Greg Kuczinski turned a very unpleasant experience into one where the big bad insurance companies had to pay for our sons suffering. Greg took our case when several other attorneys told us that a case was not possible and that if we sued we would lose. From our 1st interview/consultation Greg immediately put us at ease, assured us that we did indeed have a case and handled everything from start to finish with an unbelievable $$$$$$ finish for my son. Greg never gave up fighting with the insurance companies and wouldn’t settle with them for the pittance they were initially offering. Without going into great detail, Greg was able to get our son multi 6 figure settlement for our sons pain and suffering which made his horrible accident experience more tolerable. We knew from the start that Greg was a honest, warm, caring individual who wasn’t looking to waste our time. He spoke extensively and passionately with our son assuring him throughout this process that he would prevail financially for our son to assist with the lost wages and time spent healing from his horrible accident. We HIGHLY RECOMMEND the Law Firm of Gregory Kuczinski for all your families legal needs.

I have been a client of Gregory Kuczinski since 2008. He has represented me with both personal and business actions as plaintiff’s counsel and defendant’s counsel in both areas. Gregory Kuczinski is one of the most honest, conscientious, caring and tenacious attorneys I have ever dealt with. I highly recommend Gregory Kuczinski, whether Personal Injury, Real Estate, business or any other type of case - Gregory Kuczinski can litigate anything. He is one of the best and I am fortunate have him on my side. AAA+

The Law Office of Gregory Kuczinski, is the house counsel for my company and has represented the company regarding many issue from labor disputes, collections from delinquent clients to real estate transactions. I can unequivocally say that every experience has been met with stellar results the firm has never let us down. I would recommend them without reservation.

He took the time to explain every step of the process, answered all my questions, and made sure I always knew what was happening with my case. His calm confidence and attention to detail made an incredibly stressful situation manageable.

reviews.text: Excellent experience,  Richard Portale was awsome. Definitely recommend him and his team for any court needs!!

Before I met with Rich Portale and Kenny Calvey, I thought my son’s life was over. He was facing five different criminal cases throughout the States of New York and New Jersey along with multiple probation violations for his previous cases.  I had lost all hope and my son and I were nearly resigned to him spending the next few years in jail.  The prosecutors were not willing to offer anything less than jail and his previous attorneys told us nothing could be done to avoid jail as an outcome.  Rich and Kenny sat with us and discussed each case individually and also focused on the issues my son was going through.  It was the first time an attorney we had spoken to asked why my son was in constant trouble instead of only what he was charged with.  Rich and Kenny agreed to take on the case, but only if my son agreed to enter into treatment and follow their advice on how to improve his life and stay out of trouble in the future.  Incredibly, they uncovered issues with nearly every single one of the criminal cases, and after years of us being told there was no possible outcome in other than my son spending years in jail in his cases, all of the plea offers in his cases were reduced to just fines.  They even managed to convince the prosecutor to dismiss one of the cases entirely and for the probation department to withdraw the violation so he could finish the few months he had left.  I can honestly say that Rich and Kenny saved my son’s life, and thanks to them, my son has become a better man instead of another product of the system.  If you or a family member are facing a criminal case where you think all hope is lost, I urge you to call them, they will do everything they can to save you.  Thank you both as well as your terrific administrative staff for everything you do.
Best in the biz!!

The entire team at Denlea & Carton demonstrated genuine empathy and unwavering support throughout this journey. They made us feel valued and respected, treating our loved one with the utmost dignity. Their personalized approach made us feel like more than just clients; we felt like part of their family.

Our experience with Denlea & Carton, and John Leifert in particular, was nothing short of extraordinary. We wholeheartedly recommend Denlea & Carton to anyone seeking a personal injury law firm that genuinely cares about their clients. They turned a difficult situation into a journey of hope and resolution, and for that, we are forever grateful.

She was knowledgeable, patient, and always available to answer any questions we had at all hours. She explained each step and made sure we were well-prepared for every part of the process. We truly felt that she cared about our case and was fully committed to helping our new family be together.

Thanks to her hard work and dedication, I am now in the U.S. with my wife, and we couldn't be happier. I highly recommend her to anyone in need of an immigration attorney; she’s someone you can trust during one of the most stressful and essential times in your life.

I highly recommend The Law Office of Joana Kaso! I have had a wonderful experience with Joana. reviews.text: I’ve made a few excellent decisions in my life and using Matthew Metz’s office was one of them.  First, I’m a lawyer myself, which – in my case – means I am detailed and diligent with other lawyers.  My purchase – moving from Manhattan to Westchester – was difficult from start to finish.  The only respite from the stress was dealing with Metz’s office.  This entire office is amazing.  They move things along and keep you informed through a client platform every step of the way.  The office provides options for title services, surveys, and all other needs.  This is unlike many offices, which have cozy agreements with industry folks and drive you toward their preferred vendors.  My deal was not easy, it involved difficult sellers and a difficult seller’s attorney, who was intransigent most of the time.  I had multiple calls with Metz himself and other calls with his team.  He always took time with me, and I never felt like he was counting the billable hours.  The biggest testament to the professionalism of this office is that I used a legal plan provided through my work, which pays the office directly, and I’m certain the legal plan did not deviate from their negotiated rate for the additional hours they gave me.  We closed a difficult deal, and I couldn’t recommend this office enough.  Thank you Matt, Susan, Peter, Kathryn, Andrew and staff.  First rate.

reviews.text:
10/10 highly recommend.  Pickel Law firm fought a $20B insurance company for me & won.  They have an excellent team that delivered an excellent client experience.

I was struck in a parking lot while walking by a mini SUV speeding 15-25mph.  The vehicle hit my right hip, I flew up the windshield, and landed 10ft away on concrete, resulting in multiple bodily injuries.  The driver part was apologetic and remorseful (it was an innocent accident).

Unfortunately the $20B insurance company made my life very difficult (eg, neglected paying my medical bills, delayed issuing checks, claimed my injuries were fake), which jeopardized my health/wellbeing, medical recovery, and financial security, straining my family’s resources.

I was in a vulnerable position up against a $20B insurance company with ample resources/networks, operating within a legal system that is unfortunately heavily skewed in their favor.

The Pickel Law-firm went to bat for me, got my medical bills paid, and won a settlement amount that I believe was a good client outcome within the constraints of the law.

Alan, Elizabeth, Chris, Steve, Denise were the folks who helped me.  They’re a very experienced, capable, and professional group of people.

They navigated the bureaucratic legal system and managed my expectations very well. They knew the legal system and key players in it. They explained the laws/legal statutes to me so that I understood my recourse options.  They proactively updated me throughout the process as things evolved.  They were available for questions and heard me out when issues came up on my end.  They kept it real with me and I valued their honesty.

Importantly, they won every battle and delivered real results that improved my family’s life in a meaningful way.  We’re very grateful.

Overall, this is an excellent team that delivered an excellent client experience.

10/10 highly recommended.

Thanks for all your help.  My family and I appreciate you.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for taking the time to share your experience with us. We are thrilled to hear that our team was able to assist you in navigating through such a challenging situation. Your kind words mean a lot to us, and we are grateful for the opportunity to help. We truly appreciate your recommendation and are here for you if you ever need us again.

Initially, first thought, one of the most sophisticated, knowledgeable, and experienced firms on the legal circuit. Just would have liked a fair shake at presenting my credible med. mal. case. Moreover, have been practicing in the legal field, (19) yrs. Graduated with a 94.5%, in
the area of civil litigation alone.
Firm is not 100% sure how to determine statute of limitations of credible med. mal. case and will not take the time to do so in any capacity.... Even when patient was intentionally harmed. Continuously under the wrong standard of care during the suspension of court proceedings, under Gov. Lamont's Executive Order 7G. CT. precedent cases hold this legal safeguard, see; Connell v. Colwell, 214 Conn. 242, (1990), Sherwood v. Danbury Hospital, 252 Conn. 193, (2000).
In addition have not viewed any success cases in firms portfolio regarding med. mal., whatsoever. The firm's claim, we are the toughest law firm in the state of CT. There is nothing proprietary about this firm....

I scheduled an appointment a week in advance. On the day of the appointment, I contacted her an hour beforehand to confirm our commitment. She replied that she had decided to postpone my appointment to 4:30. In my opinion, it is unprofessional not to notify me of the change in my appointment time with adequate advance notice, considering that the appointment was paid for in advance and cost $150 USD. The day before, I decided to send her an email summarizing my case, requesting confirmation of receipt, which she didn't provide until just before the appointment. At the time of the appointment, she told me that my case was complicated and had no solution; none of the questions I asked received a clear answer.

Mr. Todd Cushner helped me at one of the most vulnerable moments in  my life. The care and dedication he gave me in my time of need were phenomenal. His knowledge of the law put my mind at ease. I would recommend him highly if you ever have the need for an bankruptcy attorney.

I recently had the pleasure of working with Todd Cushner during a challenging time in my life as I navigated through the bankruptcy process. From our initial consultation to the final resolution of my case, Mr. Cushner exhibited exceptional professionalism and expertise.

I had a very concerning experience with this attorney after paying a $500 retainer for a bankruptcy consultation while I was dealing with a housing issue. He told me he could not file bankruptcy unless I first paid one month’s rent into landlord-tenant court, but when I contacted the court they said no such requirement existed. When I asked him to clarify, he could not provide an explanation, so I requested a refund. Getting the refund required sending a certified letter, and I eventually received only $350 because $150 was deducted for a “credit report” I never received or saw evidence of. I decided to move on, but months later I began receiving invoices from the office totaling about $2,700 even though no bankruptcy was filed. During the consultation he also showed me a nude photograph he claimed another client had sent him, which I found extremely inappropriate in a professional setting. Overall, the confusing legal guidance, billing issues, and unprofessional conduct made this a very uncomfortable experience.

I was delighted with the exceptional and professional experience at this legal firm. Every detail was addressed, along with guidance and support throughout the entire process! My earnest regards to the whole team. Thank you for a quick, but diligent process.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Nelson — we respect your right to share feedback, but must keep the specifics of your case confidential. We have vigorously pursued your claim and conveyed every settlement offer; a final agreement requires both the insurer’s consent and your written approval. You have declined multiple settlement offers.

During our most recent discussions you made statements referencing violence against insurance executives and loose threats toward our staff, which will not be tolerated. We take the safety of our team seriously and are evaluating withdrawal from representation in accordance with the Rules of Professional Conduct.

If you have concerns you would like to resolve constructively, please contact attorney Michael Catallo at 914-333-0444 so we can address them in a secure, professional setting.

reviews.text: I can NOT say enough about this law firm. From day 1 they were calming  & reassuring . They explained and walked me through the process and paperwork. LUPITA was my favorite contact. She really took her time with me. She was extremely patient, kind, and always willing to help me with anything. Everything was done from day 1 via phone calls and emails (because of Covid).  With the ENORMOUS amout of paperwork issued by WC you absolutely need an attorney. Rella and Rella Associates is your answer. The attorneys are no nonsense (also helpful and informative about every hearing). Anytime I called with a question that the paralegal felt the attorney needed to answer, they would call me back promptly. They fought for everything they thought i deserved and they prevailed for me. Fabulous staff.

I am writing to express my sincere gratitude for the assistance Rella & Associates provided in securing my Social Security Disability Benefits. Your firm's efficient and effective work has made a profound difference in my life, alleviating significant financial instability and allowing me to look forward to a more secure future. I especially appreciate the speed and dedication with which you handled my case. Your professionalism and commitment to your clients are truly commendable.

reviews.text:
I reached out to Rella and Associates in mid-July 2024, after being removed from payroll in October 2023 and left without pay for over eight months. I was assigned legal aid, David Schmidt. After three months of back-and-forth emails, he finally gained access to my New York State Workers’ Comp files.

Schmidt reviewed one of my files and quickly noted the judge had “got it wrong” and that my case needed correction. Fast forward to October 3, 2024, when Michael Catallo, a senior associate, got access to all my files, and by October 4, Rella and Associates were officially retained.

Here’s where things got interesting. I discovered documents from Genero Baittiloro’s office, sent during this transition, attempting to close a case Schmidt was currently accessing—one involving $15,000 taken from my Workers’ Comp settlement. I requested that Catallo review the case, check the discrepancies, and investigate missing funds. His response, in a Brooklyn accent, was basically, “I’m not checking another lawyer’s work,” effectively denying me access to much-needed income.

About a month later, I followed up with Catallo about my next hearing. His response, paraphrased: “It’s a process; I’ll let you know.” Given that I had already been without pay for nearly a year, this casual approach wasn’t exactly reassuring. His efforts lacked urgency, so I ended the client relationship.

Overall, my experience with Rella and Associates was marked by delays, minimal progress, and what felt like a lack of commitment.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Mr. Miller,
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We are sorry to hear that your experience did not meet your expectations. We value your concerns and would like to provide some clarification regarding your case.
• Representation: You were assisted by our paralegal, David Schmidt, not assigned legal aid. Our team is dedicated to providing personalized support to all our clients.
• Access to Your File: We received access to your New York State Workers' Compensation file on October 1, 2024. Prior to the 90-day timeframe we initialed advised you.
• Retainer Agreement: The retainer was officially signed on October 3, 2024, allowing us to act on your behalf.
• Immediate Action: We promptly requested a hearing on October 4, 2024, and the Workers' Compensation Board acknowledged our filing on October 13, 2024.
• Hearing Schedule: On October 18, 2024, the Board indicated that a hearing would be scheduled in the near future. Typically, hearings are set at least 45 days after a request is filed, and this period has not yet elapsed.
• Review of Previous Legal Matters: Regarding your request to review previous legal work involving another attorney, our primary focus is to advance your current case efficiently. Delving into past unrelated matters does not further that goal nor were we retained on the matter in question. Ethically, we cannot advise you on claims we are not retained on.

We would like to clarify that we were retained for just over a month. During that brief period, we were actively working on your case, attempting to have a hearing scheduled, and advising you on the proper documentation needed moving forward.

Legal proceedings, particularly in workers' compensation, can involve inherent delays due to scheduling requirements beyond our control.

We understand that this process can be stressful, especially when it affects your livelihood. Please know that we strive to move cases forward as swiftly as possible within the guidelines set by the Workers' Compensation Board.

We regret that we were unable to meet your expectations and wish you all the best in your future endeavors.

Sincerely,
Rella & Associates

My experience with the attorney that is assigned to my workers comp case has been a total disaster. She is out of Newburgh. This attorney has refused to make phone calls and rectify the workers comp mess ( the appeal got denied to have my right shoulder surgery) and a falsified IME Report. I had to make the phone calls, write the board and force this horrible insurance company to tell me what was going on and why a second IME was scheduled, then cancelled.The excuse was the case is in litigation. I did her job! The outcome is the IME doctor is being investigated!

In addition my left arm there is swelling around 2 of the surgery anchors because of the overcompensation and I’m being sent to an IME dr that is a knee surgeon. The right arm My private insurance approved the surgery and now their billing company is involved. I sent them a lovely care package of 3 lbs with the NCA Comp disaster and my medical reports!

The attorney quit on September 4th because she wasn’t listening or comprehending to the situations or doing her job! The emails that I have prove it!! Then she was supposed get a new attorney on my case. That didn’t happen as of yet and it probably won’t! Which I do not care because I’m stuck taking care of the mess that SHE IS ALSO RESPONSIBLE FOR! I shouldn’t have had to advocate for my self! She also called my doctor a pompous ass while speaking to me on the phone back in April. If she thought that I wasn’t going to tell him or his office staff. She was sorely mistaken! Then she said she was offended that I made her call him because he was beyond livid with the injury to my right shoulder getting worse. I have never had a more unprofessional and arrogant attorney who insulted my intelligence and injuries!!! It’s absolutely disgusting!

Currently , I received a letter from my private insurance company. I filled it out and not only did I submit the letter but the records from workers comp which were an appeal denial a fraudulent IME from two years ago, a cancelled IME, and an IME that they had me seeing a knee surgeon for my shoulder( I wound up not going because I cancelled it because I had to work and we were short staffed) Nobody called me back from Genex to reschedule! But I have decided that I have nothing to prove especially when the workers comp insurance caused my right shoulder to become worse and caused an overcompensation injury. In addition, the hippa forms that I signed for my orthopedic dr that did the surgery on my left shoulder in 2021 were voided! So if I went to the IME, that drs wouldn’t have had my records to begin with!  My private insurance company also had 65 pages of my medical records for my right arm and the left overcompensation records and the surgical report! Furthermore, not only did my private insurance company say that workers comp insurance is responsible but so did the anesthesia billing company! Now I have to wait 30 days if not longer to see what they decided because I submitted over 100 pages with post it’s explaining the mess!

Update: My medical records were obtained through a voided HIPPA Release that was voided on September 16th after the attorney quit the case!  The hospital received that Hippa release on October 2nd. It was obtained by the other attorneys on October 22nd! The hospital already knows and so does the Workers Com Board. Denied that the Hippa release was voided ( I have the email) and she also never notified the board that she was no longer the attorney. I told the board it’s not my problem that she didn’t notify anyone! The incompetence is astounding and now I’m stuck fixing everything she screwed up on! If I could give negative stars I would!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Allie,

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. we truly regret that you had such a frustrating experience with our office and that you felt unsupported throughout the process. Your satisfaction and trust are of the utmost importance to us, and I want to sincerely apologize that we did not meet your expectations.

We understand how challenging dealing with workers’ compensation claims can be, especially when medical issues and legal matters are involved. We always aim to provide thorough communication and support, and I’m sorry that you felt that wasn’t the case here. Please know that we take your concerns seriously, and we are reviewing our processes to ensure that situations like this do not happen in the future.

If you are still open to it, I would welcome the opportunity to discuss your case in more detail and work toward a resolution that addresses your concerns. Please feel free to contact me directly at jobrien@foalaw.com with your phone number, and I would be happy to assist you further.

Thank you again for your feedback, and I truly hope that your situation improves moving forward.

This is an EXCELLENT LAW FIRM. My case was  against  The City of New York.  No other Law Firm was BRAVE enough to take my case. My case involved Employment Discrimination.  The word Monkey was used. I filed an EEOC Petition and Probable Cause was founded. Despite this, and MANY of my Emails that gave CREDITABLE Evidence, The City Refused to Admit Any Wrong. With the Assistance Of Loris, Tanvir and Mr. Farrow(no longer there), The City Had No Choice but to Settle this case with 6 Figures $$$. This FIRM Bows Down to NOBODY. They are PROFESSIONAL. I wanted to thank the Filippatos and team for changing my life. I reached out to your office at one of the lowest points in my career. Over the last 30 years, I have experienced various levels of bias, often feeling that I was not taken seriously, included, or given the same opportunities as others. As an African-American woman working in the retail industry, I developed different coping skills and relied on tools to stay positive and engaged.

In March 2018, when I contacted your team, I was at my lowest point due to an incident with my employer that felt different from previous experiences. Because of my past, I blamed myself, but I needed to speak to someone to share my experience and ask whether what happened was due to me or if it was an unfair, racially charged, retaliatory, and biased action. Your team was professional, empathetic, and experienced enough to understand, realize, verify, and validate that my feelings were justified and that it was indeed a racially targeted action.

After my first consultation, I knew that Filippatos and the team were the type of legal minds I wanted on my side. Thank you for your support and dedication.

I found Filippatos PLLC through a google search and I am so glad I did.  Although I was going through a difficult time, everyone at the firm made me feel comfortable. Gerry has integrity, strength of character, and drive. He even took my call at his own home after hours when our schedules did not align. Name one lawyer who would do that for a client! And all of this on what was a challenging case. Elan has excellent communication, intellect, and diligence.

reviews.text:
You know that old saying: “When it rains, it pours?” Well a few years ago, it started pouring. My husband’s health, which had been fragile for many years, started to decline. At the same time, my mother, who was by this time in her 80’s, and whom I had BEGGED for over a decade to take her meds, ask for help and do some planning (she did none of these things), made a sharp decline both physically and mentally. She wound up in the hospital. My brother and I had to scramble and try to get her life in order as well as managing her life and medical care. I didn’t even know where to start, and the stress was insane.

Thank God, a cousin recommended Attorney Ariel Rosenzveig. I am not exaggerating when I say that he and his staff have been a life saver. In this country, we are often kept in the dark at how the systems work, and what is available to us. My husband was on Medicare, so I knew a little about that. But I had no idea how Medicaid worked and in addition, how to proceed with elder care and estate planning for my mom.

Ariel walked us through all the chaos, step-by-step. If not for him, all my mother’s hard work over decades would have been wiped out. She would have ended up in some subpar nursing home with no financial cushion. She is home now, being well taken care of. All the paperwork and documentation is done.

As my husband’s health continued to deteriorate, Ariel helped us navigate finishing our own estate planning before he passed.

And after I realized that mom had started, but not completed handling her last sibling’s estate, Ariel and his staff have been walking me through that INSANE process as well.

I decided to write this review of rabbi, attorney Ely Rosenzveig to warn his clients of his perfidy, breach of fiduciary duty, and dishonesty. He was my lawyer for several years dealing with my father's estate and I trusted him because he was also a rabbi and was very charming and charismatic.

Beware of his greed. He took me to a bank to get a second mortgage on my home to pay him additional fees. Thankfully, the bank rejected me for the mortgage or I would have lost my home.

He manipulated me to pay him an exorbitant and an excessive amount of money without proper bills and while also representing an adversary whose interests were in opposition to mine.  Because of his deception, I lost a great deal of money.

Upon discovering that he was secretly representing this adversary, I reported him to the Bar Association. He couldn't dispute the facts, so he attacked my character with defamatory and unsubstantiated claims, which would not surprise me if he does once again in response to this review. He was reprimanded by the Bar Association because of his blatant breach of fiduciary duty.

I also wrote to the Israeli Bar Association as he claimed to have a law practice there at that time.  They had never heard of him. He was not actually an attorney there at that time.

I am 83 years old and disabled now and feel it is important to do this review as a caution to others.

Ariel and his staff stepped in and helped my family navigate a complicated situation when we were in the process of settling my father's estate. He has always been responsive, prompt, personable while professional, and we could not be more satisfied. In the aftermath of the loss of a beloved family member, it can be difficult to manage the legal and bureaucratic  issues that may come up, and we had complete confidence in Ariel's competence and capacity to help us through that  process.

Before I go forward, I want to explain that while no one ever expects the unexpected to happen, I found myself facing down the consequences of an unfortunate event.
And to be clear, to say that I was intimidated is an understatement.
I found myself in the crosshairs of both an unfair and an unfortunate accusation. However, after searching for the right attorney and sifting through different lawyers who either showed no concern for me or wanted far too much money, I came across the Law Offices of John Raimondo.
First, I want to explain that yes, I was nervous. As a matter of fact, I was frantic and petrified.
I was worried about what might happen and more than anything, I was worried about the cost of my personal defense.
This is when I made the call . . .
It was within minutes that I knew I found the right attorney. John discussed my case with me, both calmly and rationally. We spoke about the facts of what took place, and like a calculator, I could tell that John was figuring out the details in his mind.
My anxiety was through the roof. I was stressed out; however, each time I spoke with John, he assured me that he was taking care of my case, that I was not alone, that nothing was going to happen to me, and that he was sure he could handle my case without any issue.
John was right . . .
I can say that the trumped up charges were adjourned, contemplating a dismissal, and that as much as I worried that something might go wrong; I was twice as relieved to learn that the Law Offices of John Raimondo not only defended me, in my eyes they vindicated my name to the standing which I deserve. Thanks John!
The work you did  on my case means more to me than you'll ever  know.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Just to be clear, this is the third review (first was removed by google, second is below) from a person that was never a client. Regardless, I am submitting this response to the blatant incorrect information. It should also be noted that this reviewer, with the same last name as the one below, has a different first name which seems to indicate this person has multiple accounts so as to be able to abuse this review system and post these types of reviews under different names (she admits to being the same person).

For sake of transparency, it is true I had assumed the person this reviewer was talking about in her earlier review was someone else since the information given in that review said Westchester County, which is not where the client this reviewer is talking about was ever incarcerated. Also, yes, having been doing this for as long as I have, and having the volume of work that I have, it should not be shocking that there would be more than one person that is unhappy. Additionally, it was the reviewer that gave incorrect information that led to my prior assumption as to who she was talking about and not through any fault of my own.

There was no neglect on the appeal. This client had multiple local court cases and across those local court cases, one included a felony that was reduced to a misdemeanor and a conditional discharge was the sentence. It was later alleged the CD was violated due to new arrests. We conducted the hearing, both new arrests resulted in dismissals but the local court held him in violation of the initial CD and sentenced him to one year (the October, 2024). This is what served as the basis for the appeal, although during that time 2 new felony drug matters were charged, and then after that, a triple attempted murder case was commenced (this is the case that this client ended up having a separate lawyer on that I stayed in communication with). Prior to the attempted murder case the plan was to file the appeal on the violation of the CD and related sentence and submit a motion to stay sentencing, however, the motion became academic once the attempted murder case was commenced because of the independent custody-status on that matter. The Notice of Appeal was filed and the client was provided proof of same. I obtained an invoice for the transcripts related to the CD violation and upon the client paying, I paid the court reporter (who required payment before beginning work on transcripts). I gave proof they were paid to the client and then sent him a copy of all transcripts as they were received along with proof of the time it took to obtain them (the court reporter was back logged). Upon the attempted murder charges being added, and with the client hiring a separate lawyer on that case as it became the priority, and after speaking with the client about his finances and getting authorization from him, I filed a motion for assignment of counsel and poor person relief in the appeal and mailed the client a copy. There was no lie or negligence related to the appeal. Not only was the appeal timely filed but the motion was granted and the client was assigned an attorney in March of 2025.

The offer never went from 3 years to 9 and there was no failure to discuss plea deals/offers with the client. I had been attempting to negotiate a 3 year concurrent deal on the two separate drug matters but the People rejected that after initially considering it. They came back with 9 years. As a result the 3 was never an offer made but part of larger discussions. In that time period, the triple attempted murder was commenced. Despite having another lawyer, I was involved with working a global deal with the end result being 8 years on one drug count, concurrent with 6-12 on a conspiracy plea off the attempted murder, all of which are non-violent. The client accepted this deal and at allocution told the court he was happy with his lawyers' representation and advice and that he was not forced to take this deal.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Harmon has been a client for a number of years now resulting in a non-marital relationship ending, which resulted in litigation to divide property. When I was retained, the underlying litigation related to the property had been resolved resulting from a default against Ms. Harmon (that I had nothing to do with as I was not handling the case during that process). I made no guarantees to Ms. Harmon and advised her of her various options to attempt to vacate the default. Her argument was largely related to allegedly not being served properly. During this time, and resulting from rhe prior default before my time as her lawyer, the Court appointed a receiver. There was also the issue of return of personal property. I made the motion to vacate the default based on all the circumstances as described to me by Ms. Harmon based on an affidavit in support that she read and signed. The Court ultimately denied the motion and the property was divided. Ms. Harmon did receive her share and the property did sell for a higher value then expected.

During this process, we also negotiated the return of personal property but many items were damaged or otherwise not returned. We filed a separate lawsuit related thereto. Based upon the proof provided by Ms. Harmon to me, and after several settlement appearances and mediation/arbitration appearances an offer was made by the opposing party. Ms. Harmon was sent the various release papers and advised that if we did not agree we would proceed to trial. She was advised that based on the proof we had, it would be difficult to get more. Ultimately Ms. Harmon signed the release paperwork and the settlement funds were transferred to her. No guarantees or promises were made and she was kept apprised the entire time. Ultimately, she would have gotten nothing if not for my efforts because she initially defaulted, which I worked to mitigate. I am sorry that Ms. Harmon is not happy but the allegations in her review are wholly untrue.

Not only did he make me plea to a charge that I shouldn’t have originally pled to in new paltz but it Opened up a domino effect and he didn’t know how to handle it. reviews.ownerResponse:
Olivia came to me with existing family court orders. Regarding continuing issues with them, I assisted her in filing various petitions and defended against those filed by the father (which included allegations as to the criminal cases she references). All petitions I represented her on were either settled favorably or otherwise withdrawn and/or dismissed. Despite her criminal cases and claims of failing horribly, I was able to ensure there was no negative effect on her custody/visitation within the cases I represented her on in family court.

As to the criminal cases, Olivia clearly mentions separate cases. One was the New Paltz case. Olivia took a plea to a reduced VTL driving related offense, which was reduced from what she was initially prosecuted for. By operation of law, there was a consequence to her license that can be considered a "domino effect", although not one caused by me or any failure on my part. The plea was a bargained for disposition that led to a more favorable outcome after multiple appearances and negotiations with the DA's office. Unfortunately, there was another charge against Olivia in this time period which was made worse by the fact it occurred while certain conditions from the prior case were still in effect. This led to elevated charges on the new matter. Despite the severity of the new charges, the property damage caused, and the aggravating factors due to the occurrence happening in close proximity to the other conviction, a plea bargain was entered into after significant negotiations and conferences with multiple different courts. Olivia did serve 6 months of incarceration (a matter of public record), but only after significant plea negotiations and with no restitution despite 6 figure damages to third party real property and facing what could have easily been multiple years in state prison. I was also able to negotiate the timing of the plea to account for the birth of her new child and additional time after said birth so that sentencing did not take place until almost a year later.

Olivia speaks about missing court dates/not appearing/showing up late. At times I did not arrive to court until after the exact time it began, not uncommon for lawyers who travel from one court to the next. It's also not uncommon for lawyers to have their own emergencies and need to reschedule cases. It is normal for cases to be rescheduled as negotiations continue. This doesn't account for the numerous times I appeared in court and covered for Olivia who either couldn't find a ride or last minute advised she couldn't appear. Olivia did not appear so many times that even when I had a valid reason to request an adjournment, she would still need to appear to check in with the court. I never missed an appearance without permission from the court and if I had, there would be correspondence from the court that proved it or I would otherwise have faced public discipline or been held in contempt/sanctioned, none of which has ever happened. Despite her claims I hardly answered the telephone or responded, I have many hours' worth of texts messages and phone call records that prove otherwise.

I also don't know where the Town of Wawarsing is as I never represented Olivia there. Olivia received the benefit of a good plea considering the charges against her and her past. Also, only a couple of weeks ago Olivia called and texted me asking that I provide a letter related to her sentencing. I declined to write what she wanted because it would not have been true based upon the record. She was angry and accused me of not helping, and now we have this review.

While not everyone will be happy, this review is the result of misinformation by a person who is blaming me for consequences despite the fact I prevented much worse ones through my representation. While she has the right to speak and/or write it, it doesn't change it is untrue and that this review, as a result, is not helpful and false.

reviews.ownerResponse: While this office strives to create a positive experience for all clients, the truth is that not all clients, through no fault of this office or even them many times, will be 100% happy.  In response to Rachel's review, when she did reach out to this office, we did respond.  We spoke with Rachel about the status of her case and updates on February 3, 2020, and her case was progressing as discussed at the initial meeting.  Our Firm was moving forward with all work we were retained to perform.  Rachel last reached out for another update on February 11, 2020, and was on the schedule for a return call today.  It is important to note that this Firm is a solo practice, and Rachel was advised that any delay in a reply was due to a trial that spanned 2 weeks during this time frame.  Our office was contacted today and advised that Rachel was upset and she was contacted (which was going to happen today even without this). We had a great experience working with David Gorberg on our lemon law case. He was incredibly knowledgeable, responsive, and professional throughout. After months of getting nowhere with both the dealership and financial services, he stepped in and quickly turned things around. He was a fierce advocate, but a true gentleman; very civil and fair in his approach, which made all the difference in reaching a quick and satisfying resolution.  He kept us informed every step of the way, explained the process clearly, and always made time for our questions.  Highly recommend him to anyone dealing with a lemon vehicle--you don't have to navigate the system alone.

reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for the 5 star review! It was out pleasure helping you with your lemon law case

reviews.text: The Gorberg Firm talked us through the process of filing a lemon law complaint with Volvo. They explained all our options completely and advised us on what we needed to provide. They took care of all the paperwork and contact with Volvo. David was excellent in returning our calls and keeping us updated. We ended up up taking a settlement from Volvo and keeping the car as it is a software/ touchscreen problem that is an annoyance more than anything. I valued David’s experience and knowledge in dealing with Volvo . Would I use David Gorberg again if we ever bought another vehicle with problems that aren’t fixed? Yes absolutely.

Working with Mr. Gorberg and his staff was incredibly easy!
I highly suggest if you have any questions or concerns about your states lemon laws to reach out to them.
They got me $$$ results quickly, and effortlessly.

David is an excellent lawyer. our brand new 13 month old lease was in the dealership for 7 months when we called him. we originally had another law firm working on our case “Robinson lemon law,” specifically Courtney. They were terrible. We had to reach out to her for any updates, made us feel stupid for our questions and also told us how we do not qualify for a buyback if the dealership fixes the truck after 7 months. We knew that was not correct and called David J. gorberg & associates.
we spoke to David directly. He assured us that we can still get a buyback. He explained the process of everything and also the costs. Fast forward, David got us the buyback that we wanted. we finally do not have to deal with Chrysler and the headache of a truck.
Any questions we had David called us right away.  He was easy to get in contact with and took his time out to make sure we were well informed. On one occasion I emailed David a question and he called me to answer the question in full detail and to make sure I understood 100%. He could have easily emailed me back and called it a day,  but instead he gave me a call, which he did not have to do.
We are extremely grateful for David helping us out and extremely happy with the end result. A trustworthy lawyer who actually cares about his clients.

This company is the one to go to they helped me every step of the way and I won my dispute they kept me up to date they were very pleasant and informative it made this process very easy . Thank you David Gorberg and associates

I highly recommend David Gorberg  and associates call  1 800 MY LEMON  I am totally pleased with the results I received from Mr. Gorberg and all of his associates.especially Marie Kahlan and Jackie Murray If you feel you have a Lemon, give them a call for a free evaluation. The best decision I’ve made was hiring this firm as I got nowhere with Audi on my own. Thank you again David and associates.

They were very helpful in helping me deal with a difficult dealer and were able to get me compensated. They were also responsive throughout the entire process and I would definitely recommend them to my friends and family. Big thanks to Marie!

David Gorberg & Associates took my case and successfully won. They always answered my calls, or returned my calls immediately. They explained the procedures to where I understood every step. I would strongly recommend them to anyone who are having issues with there lemon!!

Great service. Got a car company that never settles to take back a brand new SUV I purchased that had electrical issues. Per the settlement agent "Company X never settles, you must have had a great attorney," yes, yes I did.
Excellent service. I’m glad I used this firm. They were very helpful and made the process very easy to go through. Thank you for your help.

I was contacted in August and in November, I was told the manufacturer agreed to buy it back from me. I was so relieved, I've been having too many unresolved issues with this vehicle.  I'm finally in a more reliable car but if anything happens in the future I would recommend David J. Gorberg to represent me.
